hypothesis: If a closed-loop Bayesian optimization platform autonomously explores the multidimensional parameter space of reactive carbon electrolyzers, then it will identify optimal combinations of electrolyte composition, temperature, flow rate, and current density that achieve higher CO2 reduction reactor yields more efficiently than traditional one-variable-at-a-time experimentation, because Gaussian process regression can model complex, nonlinear interactions between parameters and intelligently direct sampling toward high-performing regions of the experimental space.
